items.title: Truck torn in half after collision with train at level crossing, six injured

children.content: At least six people, including two toddlers, are injured after a train collided with a truck at a level crossing in Perth's east.

gallery.altText: A smashed train on the tracks
A damaged truck sitting near a train lines.
A damaged car sitting near train lines.

A damaged truck sitting near a train lines.
A damaged car sitting near train lines.

items.title: 'I'm over it': Minister lashes City of Perth over 'diabolical' culture

image.altText: John Carey sits with a serious contemplative expression in a TV studio. A bright beam of light shines across his face.

children.content: The "diabolical" City of Perth should be merged with its neighbouring council, a WA government minister has claimed, describing the "pettiness, infighting, immaturity" by elected members as "galling."

items.title: Former Nationals candidate refused bail over alleged hit and run

children.content: The 26-year-old allegedly struck the victim with his Toyota Hilux as he was leaving for work, causing 'catastrophic' head injuries.

items.title: Man's NDIS payments allegedly spent by homesharer on holidays, massages

children.content: A 70-year-old appointed homesharer faces court charged with 264 fraud offences after allegedly accessing the bank account of an intellectually disabled man who was living with him and spending over $54,000.

image.altText: A cllose-up shot of a man with short grey hair and a moustache, wearing spectacles and a dark polo shirt, outside court

items.title: WA authorities begin contact tracing amid warning diphtheria cases could rise

children.content: Cases of diphtheria could continue to rise as the number of people affected reaches 220 across the country — the biggest outbreak of the disease since national records began in 1991.

image.altText: A nurse injecting a needle into the arm of a patient who is getting vaccinated.

items.title: A mouse plague has broken out. Here's what you need to know

children.content: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

image.altText: A close up of dead mice laying on the brown dirt

items.title: Miners plan to retrofit WA nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices

children.content: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

image.altText: A mining executive in high-vis standing in front of a mill.

items.title: Fears mount for victims of domestic violence ahead of WA court closures

children.content: The doors of 26 regional and remote courthouses in WA will shut for three months, leaving lawyers confused and concerned about consequences for domestic violence legal matters.

image.altText: The front of a court house

items.title: 'WA Cup': Three West Aussies in Giro d'Italia top 10 after key time trial

children.content: Ben O'Connor uses his Perth experience to impress in the Giro d'Italia's only time trial stage of 2026, leapfrogging fellow West Australian Jai Hindley in the overall standings, with another Perth boy Michael Storer just behind them.

gallery.altText: Jai Hindley rides with his mouth open
Michael Storer rides his TT bike

thumbnail.altText: Jai Hindley rides with his mouth open
Michael Storer rides his TT bike

items.title: Magpies struck by deadly mystery illness as carers report declining numbers

children.content: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

gallery.altText: A mapgie pokes its head out of the towel it is wrapped in.
A sick magpie wrapped in a towel grasps a wildlife carer's finger with its claw
Close up picture of a magpie looking to the left.

items.title: Father in critical condition as police probe whether altercation with hoons led to assault

children.content: Geraldton police investigate whether complaints about hooning were behind an alleged assault that left a father-of-three in a critical condition.

image.altText: A man with a bear sits in a field and kisses a woman with long hair. She holds a little girl and a little boy looks at them.

items.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one

children.content: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

items.title: No long-term rentals but 200 short-stay listings in WA towns

children.content: There are renewed calls for a ban on Airbnbs as a new report shows holiday homes vastly outnumber permanent rentals in small regional communities.

gallery.altText: A sign that says Nannup next to a road and greenery.
A street with cars and people walking.

items.title: The men offering an alternative to 'manosphere' influencers

children.content: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

image.altText: A selfie of Caleb Ferdinand and his toddler, taken inside a car.

items.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface — as it happened

children.content: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it.

image.altText: Shares on a screen at the ASX

items.title: Balgo residents will now have to travel 11 hours to go to court

children.content: In this remote East Kimberley community, where the local court has been shut for almost two years due to security concerns, the closure of the nearest regional court from June 1 is raising concerns about justice.

gallery.altText: A map showing Balgo, Western Australia.
A rusty car without wheels sits in a desert landscape with a sign above it saying Warlayirtiri Arts Centre Balgo
a small brick courthouse with two benches outside

items.title: Stalking trial hears police officer felt 'smothered' by assistant commissioner

children.content: A WA police assistant commissioner faces trial accused of stalking a female subordinate officer and using a restricted computer system to track her movements.

image.altText: A woman with short hair and wearing a white top leaves the court after a trial.

items.title: Federal government intervenes for third time over China-linked stakes in rare earths miner

children.content: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

image.altText: A sign explaining a new mine sits in red dirt as a person walks past in boots

items.title: 'Bro, there's a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag surprise

children.content: A Kiwi couple in the town of Esperance on WA's south coast share an unlikely discovery, a live frog in a bag of Woolworths lettuce.

image.altText: A small frog in a bag of lettuce
